presentation_status_definitions.planned: The scenario is mapped to governing sources but has no checked Atlas presentation.
presentation_status_definitions.linked: The Atlas names the scenario and links an existing deterministic reference.
presentation_status_definitions.showcased: A dedicated Atlas presentation exposes checked state, viewport, and theme controls.

known_gaps: The Scenario Atlas discovery and inspection interfaces are currently English-only.
Motion follows each reference and the user motion preference; the Atlas does not claim a separate scenario motion control.

scenarios.user_job: Select an entity, inspect evidence and properties, and make a reversible decision.
scenarios.entry: An analyst opens a saved entity view or deep link.
scenarios.completion: The analyst records or exits a review decision without losing list context.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Entity list or current entity with evidence.
composition.persistent_context: Entity identity, filters, source time, and review state.

known_gaps: Selection changes and review decisions are interactive but are not URL-addressable fixtures yet.

scenarios.user_job: Compare chronology, sources, conflicts, and uncertainty before recording a finding.
scenarios.entry: An analyst opens an entity investigation from a queue or direct URL.
scenarios.completion: The analyst records an attributable finding or leaves the investigation unchanged.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Entity identity with chronology and evidence.
composition.persistent_context: Entity, source attribution, verification state, and current finding.

known_gaps: The deterministic reference has no live sources, authoritative permission service, durable finding record, or production investigation data.
Manual screen-reader, browser-zoom, and physical touch-device review remain outside the Atlas evidence.

scenarios.user_job: Triage a signal, preserve semantic separation, and assign or resolve review.
scenarios.entry: An analyst opens a risk queue with a saved scope.
scenarios.completion: The signal has an owner, reversible decision, and recorded reason.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Prioritized signal queue with evidence state.
composition.persistent_context: Queue scope, signal severity, evidence state, owner, and review status.

known_gaps: The deterministic reference has no live monitor, permission service, durable assignment, or review persistence.
Manual screen-reader, browser-zoom, and physical touch-device review remain outside the Atlas evidence.

scenarios.user_job: Inspect current measurement, history, failure scope, and retry context.
scenarios.entry: An analyst opens a monitor task from an entity or alert.
scenarios.completion: The analyst understands the last verified state and chooses retry or manual continuation.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Monitor identity, task state, and current measurement.
composition.persistent_context: Monitor identity, source time, last verified value, and retry scope.

known_gaps: Dedicated Atlas presentation and deterministic state locators are not implemented.

scenarios.user_job: Resume ranked work without inventing a generic KPI dashboard.
scenarios.entry: A returning user opens the workspace root.
scenarios.completion: The user enters a real pending task with its context intact.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Ranked work, recent context, or blocked task.
composition.persistent_context: Workspace identity, task ownership, ranking reason, and recency.

known_gaps: Runnable reference, personalization boundary, responsive behavior, and automated checks remain open.

scenarios.user_job: Search, filter, open a record, and identify its source and currency.
scenarios.entry: A reader arrives with a query, topic, or stable record URL.
scenarios.completion: The reader can identify the subject, source, update state, and next navigation step.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Search, results, or record subject.
composition.persistent_context: Query, subject, source, revision, and stable URL.

known_gaps: Search-result, empty, stale, and unavailable states are not URL-addressable in this product-pattern reference.

scenarios.user_job: Follow headings, citations, source context, and related records.
scenarios.entry: A reader opens a stable record or article URL.
scenarios.completion: The reader understands the content and can inspect or return from its sources.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Subject identity and readable content.
composition.persistent_context: Subject, author or source, revision, and location trail.

known_gaps: Citation navigation and related-record transitions are static; no selected citation or unavailable-source fixture is URL-addressable.
The deterministic reference does not fetch authoritative sources or prove consuming-product provenance.

scenarios.user_job: Understand what changed, when it changed, and why.
scenarios.entry: A reader follows a revision marker or correction link.
scenarios.completion: The reader can compare the current record with an attributable prior state.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Current revision identity and change summary.
composition.dominant_region: Revision comparison or chronological change record.
composition.persistent_context: Record identity, revision dates, editor or source, and correction status.

known_gaps: The reference exposes a concise revision summary only; diff, correction approval, rollback, and per-version deep links are not implemented.
The revision entries are deterministic local content, not an authoritative change history.

scenarios.user_job: Navigate stable records, saved views, and archived material.
scenarios.entry: A reader opens a topic, collection, or archived record.
scenarios.completion: The reader finds the intended record or understands why it is archived.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Collection identity with records or archive state.
composition.persistent_context: Collection, topic, filters, archive reason, and stable URLs.

known_gaps: Dedicated Atlas presentation and deterministic state locators are not implemented.

scenarios.user_job: Identify a blocking state, affected quantity or channel, and the next safe action.
scenarios.entry: An operator opens an exception queue or selected product.
scenarios.completion: The exception is resolved, assigned, or left with an explicit recovery path.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Actionable exception queue and selected product.
composition.persistent_context: Selection, batch scope, currency, quantity, channel, and owner.

known_gaps: Resolution, assignment, batch recovery, and rollback are not URL-addressable fixtures yet.

scenarios.user_job: Edit a product while sellable state, price, inventory, channel status, and activity stay visible.
scenarios.entry: An operator opens a product from a scoped catalog view.
scenarios.completion: The product change is saved, rejected, or left as a recoverable draft.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Product identity and sellable state.
composition.dominant_region: Product detail and editable operational fields.
composition.persistent_context: Product, channel, money, inventory, approval, and save state.

known_gaps: Save, permission, failure, and revision outcomes are deterministic local fixtures; no backend product record, authorization service, approval engine, or channel publication is connected.
The reference edits base price and available stock only; variants, tax basis, warehouse allocation, media, translations, and full activity history remain outside this fixture.

assertion.value: 本地参考已保存为修订 R19

scenarios.user_job: Review an order while payment, fulfillment, refund, ownership, and timeline remain distinct.
scenarios.entry: An operator opens an order from a queue or direct link.
scenarios.completion: The operator commits a safe fulfillment, refund, or assignment action.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Order identity with payment and fulfillment state.
composition.persistent_context: Order, customer scope, money, fulfillment, payment, and owner.

known_gaps: Dedicated Atlas presentation and deterministic state locators are not implemented.

scenarios.user_job: Review media, offer, channel, approver, and publication scope before release.
scenarios.entry: An operator opens a campaign or creative review task.
scenarios.completion: The campaign is approved, rejected, or returned with attributable feedback.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Campaign identity, readiness, and review material.
composition.persistent_context: Campaign, channel, approver, publication scope, and revision.

known_gaps: Dedicated Atlas presentation and deterministic state locators are not implemented.

scenarios.user_job: Confirm visible selection, affected count, execution result, and rollback.
scenarios.entry: An operator selects comparable records in a filtered queue.
scenarios.completion: The exact selected scope is changed or the action is safely cancelled.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Selected records and exact batch scope.
composition.dominant_region: Comparable queue with attached batch action.
composition.persistent_context: Filter, selection count, affected fields, permission, and rollback.

known_gaps: Dedicated Atlas presentation and deterministic state locators are not implemented.

scenarios.user_job: Select, modify, commit, and undo a structured object with precise context.
scenarios.entry: A user opens a document and selects an object or tool.
scenarios.completion: The change is committed to document history or safely cancelled.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Canvas with a real selection or active tool.
composition.persistent_context: Tool mode, selection, units, zoom, save state, and revision.

known_gaps: Committed, cancelled, undo, and revision-history states are interactive but are not URL-addressable fixtures yet.

scenarios.user_job: Navigate document hierarchy and inspect the current selection.
scenarios.entry: A user opens the layer or object rail in a document.
scenarios.completion: The intended object is selected, located, or structurally changed.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Document hierarchy beside the canvas.
composition.persistent_context: Document, layer hierarchy, selection, visibility, and lock state.

known_gaps: Object selection is interactive but not URL-addressable or persisted; the Atlas exposes the deterministic default hierarchy selection only.
Screen-reader tree semantics, precision input, and consuming-product hierarchy evidence remain open.

scenarios.user_job: Preview a generated diff, accept or reject it, and preserve attribution and undo.
scenarios.entry: A generated proposal is ready for document review.
scenarios.completion: The proposal is accepted into history, rejected, or left pending.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Proposed change with affected objects and scope.
composition.persistent_context: Document, proposal source, affected selection, revision, and decision.

known_gaps: Dedicated Atlas presentation and deterministic state locators are not implemented.

scenarios.user_job: Compare revisions and recover from a conflict without crowding the canvas.
scenarios.entry: A save conflict or revision comparison is opened.
scenarios.completion: The user selects, merges, or preserves a trustworthy document state.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Current document and conflicting or prior revision.
composition.persistent_context: Document, revision identities, save state, selection, and recovery action.

known_gaps: Dedicated Atlas presentation and deterministic state locators are not implemented.

scenarios.user_job: Inspect phase and progress, cancel or continue, and reach the result.
scenarios.entry: A long export or simulation operation starts.
scenarios.completion: The operation completes, moves to background, fails with recovery, or is cancelled.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Task identity, phase, progress, and affected document.
composition.dominant_region: Current document or task detail according to interruption cost.
composition.persistent_context: Document, operation, progress, cancellation, and result location.

known_gaps: Dedicated Atlas presentation and deterministic state locators are not implemented.

scenarios.user_job: Sign in, recover access, and return to the intended context.
scenarios.entry: A protected route or explicit sign-in action requires identity.
scenarios.completion: The user returns to the intended destination or receives an honest recovery path.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Current authentication step and form.
composition.persistent_context: Intended destination, identity boundary, session state, and recovery path.

known_gaps: Expired-challenge, verified, and session-expired fixtures remain available in the reference but are outside this scenario inspection set.

scenarios.user_job: Configure the product, invite collaborators, resume, and complete setup.
scenarios.entry: A new or returning user enters an incomplete setup flow.
scenarios.completion: Required setup is saved and the user reaches the first real task.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Current setup step, progress, and required decision.
composition.persistent_context: Workspace identity, progress, saved decisions, and exit or resume path.

known_gaps: Later setup steps remain navigable in the deterministic reference, but the Atlas does not equate navigation progress with persisted setup completion.
Resume state is local to the device and no workspace, import, or invitation is created by the reference.

scenarios.user_job: Search, refine, inspect a result, and restore URL state.
scenarios.entry: A user opens search directly or starts from a global query.
scenarios.completion: The user reaches a relevant result or an honest no-result or recovery state.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Query and result scope.
composition.persistent_context: Query, filters, ranking scope, selection, and URL.

known_gaps: Result selection, query, scope, access, and sort remain interactive URL state but are not represented as separate lifecycle states in the Atlas.
The deterministic reference has no search service, live ranking, or authoritative permission check.

assertion.value: Results come from a stale index

scenarios.user_job: Change settings with authorization and unsaved-change protection.
scenarios.entry: A user opens a personal, workspace, or administration setting.
scenarios.completion: The change is saved, cancelled, or preserved for recovery.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Current settings section and saved state.
composition.persistent_context: Account or workspace identity, permission, save state, and location.

known_gaps: Unsaved, saved, cancellation, and session-revocation behavior is interactive but not URL-addressable, so the Atlas exposes only the baseline profile section.
The reference does not persist profile, appearance, session, or notification changes to a backend.

scenarios.user_job: Recover from unavailable, permission, offline, or retryable state without losing useful context.
scenarios.entry: A route, request, or dependency fails or becomes unavailable.
scenarios.completion: The user retries, returns, restores context, or understands the durable boundary.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Named failure with retained useful context.
composition.persistent_context: Route, draft or cached data, failure scope, and return path.

known_gaps: The not-found fixture remains available in the reference but is outside this shared inspection vocabulary.
Recovery actions only report deterministic local feedback; they do not retry, restore a session, request access, merge, or submit data.

scenarios.user_job: Find help, inspect service status, escalate, and preserve support context.
scenarios.entry: A user opens contextual help or a support route.
scenarios.completion: The user resolves the issue, opens a durable request, or reaches a truthful status source.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Current issue, search, or support path.
composition.persistent_context: Product area, issue context, status, ticket identity, and return path.

known_gaps: Help, request, ticket, and status sections are URL-addressable navigation, but only baseline help is a lifecycle state in this tranche.
The reference creates no support ticket and connects to no authoritative status service.

scenarios.user_job: Inspect a period, select an item, and reschedule with time and selection context intact.
scenarios.entry: A user opens a saved period or selected schedule item.
scenarios.completion: The schedule change is committed, rejected by conflict, or safely recovered.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Period view or agenda with selected item.
composition.persistent_context: Period, time zone, filters, selection, and pending change.

known_gaps: Real-product time-zone, recurrence, persisted rescheduling, and assistive-technology evidence remain open.
The local reference does not commit a schedule change; the candidate source maturity remains unchanged.

scenarios.user_job: Transfer or import data through mapping, partial failure, retry, and rollback.
scenarios.entry: A user chooses a file, source, or migration job.
scenarios.completion: The import completes with an attributable result or returns to a recoverable mapping state.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Source identity, mapping task, and current phase.
composition.persistent_context: Source, destination, progress, validation, failed rows, and rollback.

known_gaps: Mapping and partial-failure reference, large-file behavior, and automated recovery checks remain open.

scenarios.user_job: Review notifications and audit records through read state, filters, detail, and retention boundaries.
scenarios.entry: A user opens a notification inbox or audit route.
scenarios.completion: The user reads, filters, follows, or records the relevant durable event.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Notification or audit list with current scope.
composition.dominant_region: Comparable event list and selected detail.
composition.persistent_context: Filters, read state, actor, time, retention, and selected event.

known_gaps: Notification inbox, audit filter/detail reference, and automated read-state checks remain open.

scenarios.user_job: Invite members, inspect roles, change permission scope, and confirm impact.
scenarios.entry: An authorized user opens organization membership or role settings.
scenarios.completion: The invitation or role change is committed with explicit scope and audit context.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Organization identity and member or invitation list.
composition.persistent_context: Organization, member, role, permission scope, and pending invitation.

known_gaps: Member and invitation reference, role-change confirmation, responsive behavior, and automated checks remain open.

scenarios.user_job: Inspect plan, usage, invoice, cancellation, or payment recovery only when the product requires billing.
scenarios.entry: An authorized user opens a real billing capability.
scenarios.completion: The user understands the plan or completes a provider-owned billing action.

composition.first_meaningful_view: Current plan, usage, invoice, or payment problem.
composition.persistent_context: Account, plan, currency, provider boundary, invoice, and recovery state.

known_gaps: Product evidence, complete contract, reference, themes, localization, interaction checks, and provider integration remain open.
